2603.16599.title: Data-driven generalized perimeter control: Zürich case study
2603.16599.paper_summary: Urban traffic congestion is a key challenge for the development of modern cities, requiring advanced control techniques to optimize existing infrastructures usage. Despite the extensive availability of data, modeling such complex systems remains an expensive and time consuming step when designing model-based control approaches. On the other hand, machine learning approaches require simulations to bootstrap models, or are unable to deal with the sparse nature of traffic data and enforce hard constraints. We propose a novel formulation of traffic dynamics based on behavioral systems theory and apply data-enabled predictive control to steer traffic dynamics via dynamic traffic light control. A high-fidelity simulation of the city of Zürich, the largest closed-loop microscopic simulation of urban traffic in the literature to the best of our knowledge, is used to validate the performance of the proposed method in terms of total travel time and CO2 emissions.
2603.16599.paper_summary_zh: 城市交通擁堵是現代城市發展的一個關鍵挑戰，需要先進的控制技術來優化現有基礎設施的使用。<lb/>儘管數據的可用性非常廣泛，但在設計基於模型的控制方法時，建模這樣複雜的系統仍然是一個昂貴且耗時的步驟。<lb/>另一方面，機器學習方法需要模擬來啟動模型，或者無法處理交通數據的稀疏性並強制執行硬約束。<lb/>我們提出了一種基於行為系統理論的交通動態新公式，並應用數據驅動的預測控制通過動態交通信號控制來引導交通動態。<lb/>我們使用了一個高保真度的蘇黎世城市模擬，根據我們的最佳知識，這是文獻中最大的閉環微觀城市交通模擬，以驗證所提方法在總旅行時間和二氧化碳排放方面的性能。
2603.16599.author: Alessio Rimoldi et.al.
2603.16599.authors: Alessio Rimoldi, Carlo Cenedese, Alberto Padoan, Florian Dörfler, John Lygeros
